perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of eighteen months from the date of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall cause its affiliates, subject to Section 22 hereof, not to), directly or indirectly, (i) use the Confidential Information to divert or attempt to divert any business or customer of the Company or (ii) employ or solicit, or initiate contact for employment with, any (A) director, officer or other senior or key employee of the Company or (B) any other employee of the Company whom the Receiving Party meets during its evaluation of the possible Transaction or about whom the Receiving Party receives Confidential 2 Information; provided , however , a general advertisement or other recruiting efforts not specifically targeting any such employees of the Company shall not be considered a solicitation or unauthorized hiring.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of twelve months from the date of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall cause its affiliates, subject to Section 22 hereof, not to), directly or indirectly, (i) use the Confidential Information to divert or attempt to divert any business or customer of the Company or (ii) employ or solicit, or initiate contact for employment with, any (A) director, officer or other senior or key employee of the Company or (B) any other employee of the Company whom the Receiving Party meets during its evaluation of the possible Transaction or about whom the Receiving Party receives Confidential 2 Information; provided , however , a general advertisement or other recruiting efforts not specifically targeting any such employees of the Company shall not be considered a solicitation or unauthorized hiring.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by altering the non-solicitation period from eighteen months to twelve months in Section 3(b), while Section 23 states the obligations terminate after eighteen months. This creates an ambiguity as to the actual duration of the non-solicitation agreement, leading to potential disputes over contractual obligations regarding employee solicitation after twelve months but before eighteen months.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as otherwise specified herein, the obligations of the parties set forth in this Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force and effect eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the non-solicitation period from eighteen months to twelve months. The contradicted text states that all obligations terminate after eighteen months. This creates a direct conflict as the non-solicitation obligation, according to the changed text, ends at twelve months, while the contradicted text suggests it should last for eighteen months, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In consideration of the Confidential Information being furnished to the Receiving Party pursuant to this Agreement, the Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of one year from the date of this Agreement (or, such shorter period agreed to by the Company with a third party who is provided access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction, the “ Standstill Period ”), unless expressly requested by the Company or its Board of Directors (or any committee thereof) in writing, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall cause its affiliates not to and shall cause its and their respective Representatives acting at its and their respective behalf not to): (a) in any manner acting alone or in concert with others, acquire, agree to acquire or make any proposal to acquire, directly or indirectly, by means of purchase, merger, business combination or in any other manner, beneficial ownership of any securities of the Company, direct or indirect rights to acquire any securities of the Company (including any derivative securities with economic equivalents of ownership of 3 any of such securities), any right to vote or to direct the voting of any securities of the Company or any assets of the Company, (b) make, or in any way participate in, directly or indirectly, any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of, any voting securities of the Company, (c) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, other than any group comprised solely of the Receiving Party and its affiliates, (d) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control, advise, change or influence the management, board of directors, governing instruments, policies or affairs of the Company, (e) make any public disclosure, or take any action that could require the Company to make any public disclosure, with respect to any of the matters set forth in this Agreement, other than the required amendment to the Receiving Party’s Schedule 13D filing as a result of the execution and delivery of this Agreement, (f) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing or (g) have any discussions or enter into any arrangements (whether written or oral) with, or advise, assist or encourage any other persons in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.changed_text: In consideration of the Confidential Information being furnished to the Receiving Party pursuant to this Agreement, the Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of two years from the date of this Agreement (or, such shorter period agreed to by the Company with a third party who is provided access to the Confidential Information for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction, the “ Standstill Period ”), unless expressly requested by the Company or its Board of Directors (or any committee thereof) in writing, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall cause its affiliates not to and shall cause its and their respective Representatives acting at its and their respective behalf not to): (a) in any manner acting alone or in concert with others, acquire, agree to acquire or make any proposal to acquire, directly or indirectly, by means of purchase, merger, business combination or in any other manner, beneficial ownership of any securities of the Company, direct or indirect rights to acquire any securities of the Company (including any derivative securities with economic equivalents of ownership of 3 any of such securities), any right to vote or to direct the voting of any securities of the Company or any assets of the Company, (b) make, or in any way participate in, directly or indirectly, any “solicitation” of “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote, or seek to advise or influence any person with respect to the voting of, any voting securities of the Company, (c) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (within the meaning of Section 13(d)(3) of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended) with respect to any voting securities of the Company, other than any group comprised solely of the Receiving Party and its affiliates, (d) otherwise act, alone or in concert with others, to seek to control, advise, change or influence the management, board of directors, governing instruments, policies or affairs of the Company, (e) make any public disclosure, or take any action that could require the Company to make any public disclosure, with respect to any of the matters set forth in this Agreement, other than the required amendment to the Receiving Party’s Schedule 13D filing as a result of the execution and delivery of this Agreement, (f) disclose any intention, plan or arrangement inconsistent with the foregoing or (g) have any discussions or enter into any arrangements (whether written or oral) with, or advise, assist or encourage any other persons in connection with any of the foregoing.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 6 extends the Standstill Period from one year to two years, contradicting the general termination clause in Section 23 which states that all obligations terminate after eighteen months. This creates confusion on whether the standstill provision is enforceable beyond the initial eighteen-month period, raising questions about the extent of the Receiving Party's obligations regarding acquisition and influence over the Company during the extended period from eighteen months to two years.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as otherwise specified herein, the obligations of the parties set forth in this Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force and effect eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text extends the Standstill Period from one year to two years. The contradicted text states that all obligations terminate eighteen months from the date of the agreement. Therefore, the extension of the standstill provision directly contradicts the termination clause, creating a strong in-text contradiction because it is unclear whether the standstill provision is enforceable beyond the eighteen-month period, as it should be according to the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents or materials prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect any Confidential Information shall be destroyed. Notwithstanding the return to the Company or destruction of Confidential Information pursuant to this Section 7, the Receiving Party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their 4 confidentiality obligations and other obligations under this Agreement for the term hereof. Notwithstanding the foregoing, (a) the Receiving Party’s legal department and/or outside counsel may keep one copy of the Confidential Information (in electronic or paper form) and, with respect to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who are accounting firms, such firms may keep one copy of the Confidential Information, in each case, if required to comply with applicable law or regulation and (b) the Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain Confidential Information to the extent it is “backed-up” on its or their (as the case may be) electronic information management and communications systems or servers, is not available to an end user and cannot be expunged without considerable effort; provided, that any such information so retained pursuant to clauses (a) and (b) of this Section 7 shall be held in compliance with the terms of this Agreement for a period of eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents or materials prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect any Confidential Information shall be destroyed. Notwithstanding the return to the Company or destruction of Confidential Information pursuant to this Section 7, the Receiving Party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their 4 confidentiality obligations and other obligations under this Agreement for a period of thirty-six months. Notwithstanding the foregoing, (a) the Receiving Party’s legal department and/or outside counsel may keep one copy of the Confidential Information (in electronic or paper form) and, with respect to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who are accounting firms, such firms may keep one copy of the Confidential Information, in each case, if required to comply with applicable law or regulation and (b) the Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain Confidential Information to the extent it is “backed-up” on its or their (as the case may be) electronic information management and communications systems or servers, is not available to an end user and cannot be expunged without considerable effort; provided, that any such information so retained pursuant to clauses (a) and (b) of this Section 7 shall be held in compliance with the terms of this Agreement for a period of eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: In Section 7, the confidentiality obligations are extended to thirty-six months, but the retention period for backed-up information remains at eighteen months. This creates an in-text contradiction because the Receiving Party is obligated to maintain confidentiality for thirty-six months, but only required to protect retained/backed-up confidential data for eighteen months. This introduces ambiguity regarding what constitutes compliance with the confidentiality obligation during the period between eighteen and thirty-six months.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Promptly upon the written request of the Company, the Receiving Party will return all copies of the Confidential Information to the Company, and all notes, studies, reports, memoranda and other documents or materials prepared by the Receiving Party or its Representatives that contain or reflect any Confidential Information shall be destroyed. Notwithstanding the return to the Company or destruction of Confidential Information pursuant to this Section 7, the Receiving Party and its Representatives will continue to be bound by their 4 confidentiality obligations and other obligations under this Agreement for a period of thirty-six months. Notwithstanding the foregoing, (a) the Receiving Party’s legal department and/or outside counsel may keep one copy of the Confidential Information (in electronic or paper form) and, with respect to the Receiving Party’s Representatives who are accounting firms, such firms may keep one copy of the Confidential Information, in each case, if required to comply with applicable law or regulation and (b) the Receiving Party and its Representatives may retain Confidential Information to the extent it is “backed-up” on its or their (as the case may be) electronic information management and communications systems or servers, is not available to an end user and cannot be expunged without considerable effort; provided, that any such information so retained pursuant to clauses (a) and (b) of this Section 7 shall be held in compliance with the terms of this Agreement for a period of eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 7 to extend the confidentiality obligations to thirty-six months, while the retention period for backed-up information remains at eighteen months. This creates an in-text contradiction because the Receiving Party is obligated to maintain confidentiality for thirty-six months, but is only explicitly required to protect retained/backed-up confidential data for eighteen months. The explanation accurately reflects that the modification conflicts with the contradicted text, which is the same text, creating an inconsistency regarding the duration of data protection obligations versus the overall confidentiality obligations, which aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: Except as otherwise specified herein, the obligations of the parties set forth in this Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force and effect eighteen months from the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as otherwise specified herein, the obligations of the parties set forth in this Agreement shall terminate and be of no further force and effect twenty-four months from the date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the termination clause in Section 23 to twenty-four months, while the non-solicitation agreement in Section 3(b) remains at eighteen months, an in-text contradiction emerges. This creates an ambiguity regarding the enforceability of other obligations, such as confidentiality, during the period between eighteen and twenty-four months. It becomes unclear whether the Receiving Party is still bound by confidentiality obligations after eighteen months, despite the general termination date being set at twenty-four months.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees that, for a period of eighteen months from the date of this Agreement, the Receiving Party shall not (and shall cause its affiliates, subject to Section 22 hereof, not to), directly or indirectly, (i) use the Confidential Information to divert or attempt to divert any business or customer of the Company or (ii) employ or solicit, or initiate contact for employment with, any (A) director, officer or other senior or key employee of the Company or (B) any other employee of the Company whom the Receiving Party meets during its evaluation of the possible Transaction or about whom the Receiving Party receives Confidential 2 Information; provided , however , a general advertisement or other recruiting efforts not specifically targeting any such employees of the Company shall not be considered a solicitation or unauthorized hiring.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified an 18-month termination. The changed text extends this to 24 months. The contradicted text defines a non-solicitation agreement that lasts for 18 months. By extending the overall termination to 24 months while the non-solicitation agreement remains at 18 months, the modified text creates a direct inconsistency regarding obligations between months 18 and 24, constituting a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement represents the entire understanding and agreement of the parties hereto with respect to the matters contained herein, and may be amended, modified or waived only by a separate writing executed by the Receiving Party and the Company expressly so amending, modifying or waiving this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement represents the entire understanding and agreement of the parties hereto with respect to the matters contained herein, and may be amended, modified or waived only by a separate writing executed by the Receiving Party, or orally agreed upon and documented by both the Receiving Party and the Company amending, modifying or waiving this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying Section 14 to allow for oral amendments if properly documented, a contradiction is introduced with Section 14, which requires written amendments. The change creates ambiguity as to whether oral agreements can effectively change the terms of the agreement and which amendment will be applicable and enforcable, leading to uncertainty about what actually constitute valid modifications of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement represents the entire understanding and agreement of the parties hereto with respect to the matters contained herein, and may be amended, modified or waived only by a separate writing executed by the Receiving Party, or orally agreed upon and documented by both the Receiving Party and the Company amending, modifying or waiving this Agreement. This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of and be binding upon the parties and their respective successors and assigns.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that amendments, modifications, or waivers must be in writing. The changed text introduces the possibility of oral agreements if documented, creating a direct contradiction with the requirement for written amendments. The contradicted text is a repetition of the Changed Text, further solidifying the contradiction created between the two clauses in the same section of the document, fitting the definition of Inconsistencies.

perturbation.original_text: Each of the parties hereto acknowledges that in the event of any breach of the terms of this Agreement, the other party could not be made whole by monetary damages only. Accordingly, each of the parties hereto, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled in law or in equity, shall be entitled to an injunction (which shall include a temporary restraining order) to prevent breaches of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of the parties hereto acknowledges that in the event of any breach of the terms of this Agreement, the other party could not be made whole by monetary damages only. Accordingly, each of the parties hereto, will only be entitled to monetary damages, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled in law or in equity.
perturbation.explanation: By changing Section 11 to eliminate the right to injunctive relief and only allowing monetary damages, while Section 11 states that each party is entitled to an injunction (which shall include a temporary restraining order) to prevent breaches of the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of the parties hereto acknowledges that in the event of any breach of the terms of this Agreement, the other party could not be made whole by monetary damages only. Accordingly, each of the parties hereto, will only be entitled to monetary damages, in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled in law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 11 to state that parties are 'only entitled to monetary damages', while the original text and contradicted text states that they are entitled to 'an injunction'. This creates a direct conflict because the contradicted text says parties can pursue injunctive relief, while the changed text explicitly limits them to only monetary damages, making it a strong in-text contradiction as described in the explanation.
